description: As part of join work between Atlas Axis and Core Facilitator teams, this pull request migrates the Atlas from HTML to Markdown. This allows us to express the full hierarchical structure of the Atlas and will support future automations and AI integrations that are under development.

diff: For instance, this risk-capital acquisition strategy could involve:
1. Renting Junior Risk Capital (JRC)
◦ Sourcing Prime-External Junior Risk Capital (SEJRC) by renting it from other Primes through Ecosystem Accords.
◦ Sourcing Tokenized External Junior Risk Capital (TEJRC) from external capital providers.
2. Sourcing Senior Risk Capital (SRC):
◦ Originating SRC through the Sky monthly auction.
◦ Renting OSRC from other Primes through Ecosystem Accords.
3. Increasing Internal Junior Risk Capital (IJRC): A Prime might have plans to bolster its own IJRC through retained earnings or other internal means.
4. Decreasing exposure in an existing Instance(s) to lower Aggregate RRC.
description: Extra spaces have been removed. --- The following have been kept unchanged: - Code blocks/lines have been kept unchanged excepting the removal of: - Line-final spaces - Interlinear spaces - Table layouts excepting the removal of: - Line-final spaces Newlines have not been changed.

2. Sourcing Senior Risk Capital (SRC):

3. Increasing Internal Junior Risk Capital (IJRC): A Prime might have plans to bolster its own IJRC through retained earnings or other internal means.

description: @blimpa @Coolcatcatio This PR updates all lists within the Atlas to follow the markdown specification. It also adds a markdownlint config file to start the process of fixing formatting across editors.

diff: As a critical component of the Capital & Operational Plan, the Prime Agent must articulate its strategy for ensuring its Total Risk Capital (TRC) adequately covers the pro-forma Instance RRC of a new or modified Instance and the resulting impact on its Aggregate RRC.

The Prime’s notional TRC coverage strategy must outline how this new Instance’s RRC will be notionally accounted for or supported by the Prime’s overall TRC pool. If the Prime’s current TRC is not sufficient to cover its estimated increased Aggregate RRC, the strategy should
outline the Prime Agent’s intended steps for acquiring additional JRC/SRC.
For instance, this risk-capital acquisition strategy could involve:
1. Renting Junior Risk Capital (JRC)
- Sourcing
Prime-External Junior Risk Capital (SEJRC)
by renting it from other Primes through Ecosystem Accords.
- Sourcing
Tokenized External Junior Risk Capital (TEJRC)
from external capital providers.
2. Sourcing Senior
Risk Capital (SRC):
- Originating SRC
through the Sky monthly auction.
- Renting OSRC
from other Primes through Ecosystem Accords.
3. Increasing Internal Junior Risk Capital (IJRC):
A Prime might have plans to bolster
its own IJRC through retained earnings or other internal means.
4. Decreasing exposure in an existing Instance(s) to lower Aggregate RRC.

prTitle: Atomic Atlas: decompose Sky Atlas.md into one-folder-per-document tree + sync tooling
